etymology.description: semantic 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 𠂤(𠂤) (duī) -> movement after something -> pursue
etymology.interpretation: The character 追(zhuī, "pursue") combines the semantic element 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") representing motion, with the phonetic 𠂤(𠂤) which provides the sound. The combination implies the idea of moving after something, which logically extends to the sense of pursuing or chasing.

examples.chinese: 他决定追求自己的梦想(他決定追求自己的夢想)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng zhuīqiú zìjǐ de mèngxiǎng.
examples.english: He decided to pursue his dreams.

examples.chinese: 小狗在花园里追着蝴蝶(小狗在花園裡追著蝴蝶)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo gǒu zài huāyuán lǐ zhuīzhe húdié.
examples.english: The puppy is chasing butterflies in the garden.

examples.chinese: 我正在追逐更好的生活(我正在追逐更好的生活)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhèngzài zhuīzhú gèng hǎo de shēnghuó.
examples.english: I am pursuing a better life.
